subject: Accounting
title: Financing Cash Flow
description: A fast breakdown of Financing Cash Flow for builders.

hook.text: A startup raises $20 million from venture capitalists. Where does that show up? Financing.

buildup.text: Financing activities track cash from or to investors and lenders.

discovery.text: Issuing stock, borrowing money, repaying loans, and paying dividends all live here.

twist.text: Heavy borrowing can fuel growth—or signal that operations can't sustain the business alone.

climax.text: Mature companies often show negative financing flow—they're returning cash to shareholders.

punchline.text: Financing shows where the money came from—and where it went back.

quiz.question: What does financing cash flow include?
options: Borrowing, issuing stock, and paying dividends
